cycleContext.note: June 1 scan (24-hour window since May 31 cycle). Key updates: (1) Iran: 2 Kurdish political prisoners secretly executed June 1 (Maleki, Mohammadinia); 600+ executions in 2026 YTD; 2 more executed over mosque attack June 1; (2) DRC/Uganda Ebola: DRC 321 confirmed cases/48 deaths (up from 282/42); Uganda 15 cases/1 death now including Kampala; WHO PHEIC declared May 17; (3) Hungary: constitutional amendment formally launched June 1 after Sulyok refused to resign; framework law mechanism; ~1 month process; (4) West/Central Africa lean season now active: 52.8M food insecure June-August; Nigeria Borno catastrophic first time in decade; (5) Sudan lean season begins: 19.5M food insecure, 135,000 IPC Phase 5; only 20% funded; (6) Oracle WARN Act final phase June 15: sign-release-or-forfeit-severance active; (7) Bolivia: 31+ day blockade continues, 89 blockade points, mediation stalled; (8) India: 1,500+ Bengali Muslims expelled without due process including Indian citizens; 40 Rohingya thrown into sea; (9) Tesla EEOC mediation June 17 deadline; (10) Afghanistan: Taliban Decree No. 18 child marriage codified; UNAMA confirmed May 21.

pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.status: ACTIVE: June 15 is final termination date. Sign-release-or-forfeit-severance phase reached June 1. Workers denied better terms. WARN Act classification challenges ongoing. Assessment warranted before June 15.

pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.signal: DoD agreement published; assessment outstanding since April 18
pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.status: OVERDUE: 44+ days since April 18 baseline. DoD agreement amended but enforcement contested by Lawfare. Kalinowski resigned March 7. Protest letters, mission-safety-word deletion, PBC conversion all unresolved. Assessment required immediately.
ONGOING: 31+ day blockade. 89 active blockade points. Mediation stalled. Military deployment law in force. No resolution in sight. Economic crisis structural cause unresolved.
ESCALATING: DRC 321 cases/48 deaths; Uganda 15 cases/1 death including Kampala. WHO PHEIC. No vaccine. Cross-border spread accelerating. Assessment warranted.

pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.signal: DC Circuit divided on Pentagon supply-chain-risk appeal
pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.status: ACTIVE: No ruling yet. DC Circuit divided at May 2026 oral arguments. RSP v3.0 dropped pre-commitment pledge. Pentagon designation remains in place. Positive governance signal (refusal of unrestricted access) pending full assessment.

NEW: Amendment formally initiated June 1. Tisza Party 2/3 majority. Framework law. ~1 month to completion. Most significant ACC governance restructuring since Magyar took power. Assessment now warranted.

pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.signal: 2 Kurdish political prisoners secretly executed June 1; 600+ executions in 2026 YTD
pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.status: NEW ESCALATION: Executions of January 2026 protesters accelerating. 123rd week of prison hunger strikes. New death sentences. Assessment warranted to update execution-machine signal.

pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.signal: 1,500+ Bengali Muslims expelled to Bangladesh without due process including Indian citizens
pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.status: NEW: HRW confirmed 40 Rohingya thrown into sea from navy vessel. 10,000+ Gujarat demolitions against Supreme Court ruling. Pattern of systematic targeting of Muslim minorities. Assessment warranted.

DRC: Ebola Bundibugyo 321 confirmed cases/48 deaths; Uganda 15 cases including Kampala; WHO PHEIC declared May 17; CDC mobilized international response
Hungary: constitutional amendment formally launched June 1 after Sulyok refused to resign; Tisza Party 2/3 majority; framework law mechanism; ~1 month process
West/Central Africa lean season now active: 52.8M people facing acute food insecurity June-August; Nigeria Borno state catastrophic hunger for first time in decade
Sudan lean season begins June: 19.5M food insecure, 135,000 IPC Phase 5 catastrophe, 825,000 children severe acute malnutrition expected; only 20% funded
Oracle WARN Act final phase reached June 1: sign-release-or-forfeit-severance active for 30,000 workers through June 15; workers denied better terms
India: 1,500+ Bengali-speaking Muslims expelled to Bangladesh without due process May-June; 40 Rohingya thrown into sea from navy vessel; Gujarat demolitions against Supreme Court ruling
Tesla EEOC racial discrimination mediation: June 17, 2026 deadline reached; if unresolved, next-phase protocol due June 17
Afghanistan: Taliban Decree No. 18 (May 14) codifies child marriage — UNAMA confirmed May 21 permits treating girl silence as consent
Somalia: 6M (31%) facing IPC Phase 3+ food insecurity; Burhakaba District IPC Phase 5 with famine risk; 2026 HNRP only 15.2% funded
South Sudan: 7.8M (56% of population) acutely food insecure; 73,300 in IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe (160% increase); Luakpiny/Nasir at famine risk
EU AI Act August 2, 2026 enforcement deadline now 62 days away: transparency obligations, GPAI rules, Commission enforcement powers all begin

prioritizedEntities.name: Uganda
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo confirmed: 15 cases including in Kampala capital, 1 death. Cross-border spread from DRC. CDC mobilized international response. WHO PHEIC declared May 17. Crisis combines pre-existing human rights concerns (anti-homosexuality law) with new public health emergency.

prioritizedEntities.name: Palestine
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: OCHA April 23 report: humanitarian needs deepening, aid access constrained. Reconstruction needs $71.4B vs <$1B delivered. 77% of population (1.6M+) facing high levels of acute food insecurity. 37 INGOs banned. Gaza economy contracted 84%. Recovery National Committee framework established. Palestine last assessed May 17.

prioritizedEntities.name: Oracle Corporation
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: WARN Act final-phase terminations active through June 15. TechTimes June 1: sign-release-or-forfeit-severance phase reached for 30,000 laid-off workers. Workers tried to negotiate better severance; Oracle declined. 14 workers allege remote classification to reduce WARN obligations. New Jersey state WARN separately filed. Composite 20.6 sits 0.6pt above Critical boundary.

prioritizedEntities.name: India
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: HRW confirmed 1,500+ Bengali-speaking Muslims expelled to Bangladesh May-June 2026 without due process; 192 registered UNHCR Rohingya expelled despite refugee status; 40 allegedly thrown into sea near Myanmar from navy vessel. Gujarat: 10,000+ structures demolished against Nov 2024 Supreme Court ruling. India-Pakistan ceasefire (from May 2025 conflict) holding.

prioritizedEntities.name: South Sudan
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: HRW May 6: civilians starving in conflict areas. IPC: 7.8M (56% population) acutely food insecure April-July 2026; 73,300 in IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe (160% increase). Luakpiny/Nasir County at famine risk. 142,000 displaced from Akobo County; 100,000 fled to Ethiopia. WFP requires $266M urgently. Fighting between SAF and SPLM-IO escalated.

prioritizedEntities.name: Anthropic
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: DC Circuit appellate judges divided at May 2026 oral arguments. Judge Henderson: no evidence supports supply-chain-risk designation. Judge Rao: court should not second-guess Hegseth. No ruling date. Anthropic holds position refusing DoD "all lawful purpose" terms. RSP v3.0 (Feb 24) dropped pre-commitment pledge, drew safety advocate criticism.

prioritizedEntities.name: CVS Health
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: 340B lawsuit active: Mount Sinai, University of Kansas, University of Michigan hospitals filed May 21 alleging CVS/CaremarkPCS/WellPartner diverted $250M in 340B drug savings 2020-2025. CVS filed to challenge Tennessee PBM reform law. Louisiana earlier settled for $45M. Structural role in drug access for low-income patients at issue.

prioritizedEntities.name: UnitedHealth Group
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: UnitedHealth confirmed via SEC 10-Q FY2026 compliance with DOJ criminal and civil formal requests. Investigation: Medicare Advantage billing practices and Medicare fraud. AI claim-denial class action in discovery phase. CEO Thompson murder (Dec 2024) trial ongoing. ~50 insurers signed voluntary prior-auth streamlining pledge June 2026.

prioritizedEntities.name: Bolivia
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Bolivia general strike now in week 5+ (since May 5). 31+ consecutive days of blockades, 89 active blockade points across 6 departments. Ombudsman: 7 dead, 23 wounded, 321 arrested; Victor Cruz Quispe killed May 23. President Paz signed military deployment law May 27. Mediation stalled. Food/fuel/medicine shortages worsening.

prioritizedEntities.name: Israel
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: HRW May 19: Israel curbs aid and kills civilians during ceasefire. Feb 28 crossing closure brought trucks from 4,200 to 590/week; only 2 crossings operational. 37 INGOs banned. Gaza economy contracted 84%; 370,000+ housing units destroyed; reconstruction needs $71.4B vs less than $1B delivered. Six months into ceasefire, aid access remains catastrophically inadequate.

prioritizedEntities.name: Hungary
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Constitutional amendment process formally launched June 1 after Sulyok refused to resign. Magyar/Tisza Party with 2/3 majority initiated procedures. Framework law mechanism (not personal) — unprecedented in Hungarian history. Process takes ~1 month. Sulyok consulted Venice Commission. Confirmed by Bloomberg, WashPost, Euronews, AlJazeera.

prioritizedEntities.name: Nigeria
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: West/Central Africa lean season active June-August. Nigeria accounts for major share of 52.8M facing acute food insecurity. 15,000 people in Borno State at risk of catastrophic hunger for first time in nearly a decade. WFP launched lean season response but severely underfunded. Nigeria last assessed May 13.

prioritizedEntities.name: Democratic Republic of C
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak escalating: DRC 321 confirmed cases, 48 deaths; Uganda 15 confirmed cases including in Kampala capital, 1 death. WHO PHEIC declared May 17. No approved vaccine. CDC mobilized international response. Aid cuts, conflict, and misinformation hampering response. 26.5M facing acute hunger in DRC.

prioritizedEntities.name: Iran
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Iran secretly executed two Kurdish political prisoners Ashkan Maleki and Mehrdad Mohammadinia on June 1, 2026 at Ghezel Hesar Prison. 600+ executions in 2026 YTD including January uprising protesters. "No to Executions Tuesdays" campaign in 123rd week across 56 prisons. Euronews June 1: two more executed over alleged mosque attack.

prioritizedEntities.name: Sudan
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Lean season June-September now active: 19.5M facing crisis food insecurity, 135,000 in IPC Phase 5 catastrophe. 825,000 children under 5 expected severe acute malnutrition in 2026. 2026 plan only 20% funded. Famine confirmed in Al Fasher and Kadugli; 20 additional famine-risk areas. Civil war entering third year; no peace process.

sectorAlerts.description: WHO declared PHEIC May 17. DRC 321 confirmed cases (+39 in 2 days), 48 deaths; Uganda 15 cases now including Kampala capital, 1 death. No approved vaccine. Aid cuts, conflict, and misinformation hampering response. Cross-border spread accelerating.

Iran executed 2 Kurdish political prisoners June 1 in secret (Maleki, Mohammadinia). 600+ executions in 2026 YTD. 2 more executed June 1 over alleged mosque attack. "No to Executions Tuesdays" in 123rd week. New death sentences handed down to protest participants.

June-August 2026 lean season now active. 52.8M people facing acute food insecurity in West and Central Africa. Nigeria Borno catastrophic (first time in decade). Mali, Niger, Chad, Cameroon at crisis levels. WFP severely underfunded for lean season response.

sectorAlerts.description: Magyar launched constitutional amendment process June 1 to remove President Sulyok. Framework law mechanism (not personal). Tisza Party 2/3 majority. First use of constitutional removal in Hungarian history. Venice Commission consulted by Sulyok. ~1 month to complete.

Sudan enters lean season June-September with 19.5M food insecure and 135,000 in IPC Phase 5 catastrophe. 2026 plan only 20% funded. 825,000 children under 5 expected severe acute malnutrition. Famine confirmed in Al Fasher and Kadugli. 20 additional famine-risk areas.

Oracle WARN Act terminations active through June 15. 30,000 workers in sign-release-or-forfeit-severance phase. Workers denied better terms. 14 WARN Act classification challenges. New Jersey state WARN separately filed. Composite 20.6 is 0.6pt above Critical boundary.

Pentagon AI polarization: Anthropic DC Circuit hearing divided. OpenAI amended DoD deal but enforcement contested. xAI Grok in classified systems with no ethical constraints. DeepMind UK union now formed. EU AI Act transparency enforcement begins August 2, 2026.

Gujarat: 10,000+ structures demolished against Supreme Court ruling. Escalating systematic targeting of Muslim minorities.
